response.reasoning: Role requires 15 yrs experience, director-level IC, recognized AI expertise, cloud/AI platform strategy, advising strategic customers, and inspiring at scale. Candidate has ~16 yrs of software experience (since ~2009), strong full-stack and polyglot background (TypeScript, Node, DLang, C/C++, Java), founder of an open-source GIS platform, and recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling at re:cinq (only since April 2025). However, the resume shows Senior/Staff-level IC roles, not Principal/Director-level scope. No evidence of recognized AI expertise, no published AI products/platforms, no Master's/PhD, limited cloud-scale strategy work, and no track record advising strategic customers/partners. Title says 'Senior Software Developer' — a significant gap from Principal/Director IC. Cloud experience exists (AWS CDK, Kubernetes) but not at platform-strategy level. AI work is recent and tooling-focused, not product/platform leadership.
strengths: ~16 years of polyglot engineering across web, mobile, embedded, and high-throughput backends
Founder of GISCollective, demonstrating long-term ownership and open-source product delivery
Recent hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators
concerns: No recognized AI expertise, publications, or shipped AI products/platforms at scale
Career level is Senior/Staff IC, not Principal/Director with strategic influence
Limited evidence of advising strategic customers or driving cloud/AI platform strategy

key_factors.factor: Recognized AI expertise and shipped AI products
Seniority gap vs. Principal/Director-level IC scope
Long polyglot engineering tenure with founder experience

response.justification: The candidate has solid full-stack engineering depth and meets the 15-year experience bar, with recent dabbling in AI-agent tooling and a founder track record. However, the role demands a Principal/Director-level IC with recognized AI expertise, demonstrated AI products or platforms, and a history of advising strategic customers — none of which are evidenced. The AI exposure is recent and infrastructural rather than product/platform leadership, and prior titles cap at Senior/Staff. Fit is partial; a screening conversation could clarify AI depth, but the bar for this role is unlikely to be met.
